2ec792f00eb812df.description: a sunlit hotel room on the wedding morning, a garment bag and bouquet waiting on the made bed, a vanity mirror ringed with half-used makeup and a phone screen glowing face-up between two untouched coffee cups.

7a261b5fa95e2ea4.description: a cramped bridal-shop back room after closing hours, one bare bulb swinging over pin cushions and fabric scraps, the wedding dress glowing white on its mannequin while three angled mirrors throw the scene back at itself.

dd77f5fd77a7178c.description: wide stone steps under a crush of press cameras and flashbulbs, the public stage where the frame-up plays out for the whole city to see

22814f7cdfc2cb16.description: a all-night neon-lit diner booth where off-duty detectives unwind, its warm red vinyl a rare splash of comfort in the show's world

5ee336730bdc40ff.description: a stark, gray box with a scarred metal table and a one-way mirror throwing back distorted reflections

4e808f019a09a639.description: a cramped, fluorescent-lit detective floor cluttered with case boards and cold coffee, its harsh overhead light making every tired face look worse than it is

5b74c0496c4933d2.description: a once-warm apartment now half-consumed by red string and pinned crime-scene photos spreading from Reid's home office into the living room

962ea2709fd629b3.description: a fog-shrouded park bench above the harbor where cryptic notes and dead drops change hands under sodium streetlight

6d1881dea68f3013.description: a hidden, book-lined room lit by a single green banker's lamp, every object arranged with obsessive, museum-like precision

6d0bbe99406cc32b.description: a gutted, moon-lit industrial ruin the Curator uses as his private gallery, rusted machinery looming over the tableaux he leaves behind

1722cd144f1ae575.description: A cluttered municipal office humming with server racks, a wall of monitors cycling through traffic-cam feeds from across the city.

6240bf7f086d4b33.description: Crestline Chauffeur Service's control room, a wall of monitors tracking the fleet's GPS pings under buzzing fluorescent light, radios murmuring over coffee-stained desks.

c1c4f1f2670fe984.description: An abandoned riverside warehouse with its collapsed east wing still fenced off, rusted machinery inside and old memorial photos taped to the chain-link outside.

cc7c427fc76e84d3.description: Marcus's cramped apartment above a laundromat, peeling wallpaper and a single window facing the elevated train tracks.

361e559dcef4c64b.description: The armored back-and-front cabin of a black luxury sedan, dashboard screens glowing an eerie blue, rain streaking the sealed windows, a locked partition between the front and back seats.

43eb99721694fd4b.description: A glass-walled executive office forty floors up, city lights sprawling below, all steel and marble and cold minimalism.

4619834e8ddc6174.description: a cramped antiquarian bookshop thick with dust and lamplight, shelves leaning close overhead, and a locked back room no customer has ever seen

190bbd8dfcf99dc5.description: a neon-lit 24-hour diner with cracked red vinyl booths, the one place detectives go to say what they can't say at the precinct

f61779d882950400.description: a rusted industrial waterfront wrapped in river fog, shipping containers stacked like a maze, chosen again and again as the killer's stage

68dd01b5a82b7ea2.description: a small, warmly lit corner grocery with family photos taped behind the register, its windows increasingly marked by graffiti as suspicion spreads

de63fe7beaf7e456.description: a fluorescent-lit bullpen choked with corkboards, red string, and coffee-stained case files under a permanent hum of overworked ceiling fans

84b9eba85fc05363.description: a warm suburban home slowly going cold — family photographs on every wall, one side of the bed increasingly, conspicuously undisturbed
